(transitive) to feed (someone or something), to provide with food, (transitive) to feed (a person or animal) (+ m: with (a food)) [since the Old Kingdom], (transitive) to fatten, to force-feed (poultry) by gavage [Old Kingdom], (transitive) to ‘feed’ (gods or the dead) by providing offerings of food; to sacrifice food to; likely cognate with arabic سَنَّ • (sanna) “to sharpen, to whet, to hone, to grind, to mold, to shape, to form, to prescribe, to introduce, to enact, to institute, to establish a method سَنَّ (sanna, “to establish (a law, custom, etc.)”).”/ 2. سَنَة • (sana) “year” From Proto-Semitic *šanat- ”year,season”, compare Hebrew שָׁנָה, 𒈬 mu Akk. šattu/šattum./3.  Ugaritic 𐎌𐎐𐎚 (šnt) “year, sleep” From Proto-Semitic *šinat- (“sleep”). Mount of Kankaniya 𐎋𐎐𐎋𐎐𐎊 (knkny) possible logographic meaning, logographically composed of the signs: -  suggested root is arabic knn- كِنّ • (kinn) m (plural أَكْنَان (ʔaknān)), veil, envelope, cover, house, asylum, refuge; comparable to akkadian kannu (sumer. ganum) 𒄑𒃶/𒄑𒃶𒉡 "(vessel-)stand; a large vessel; a small container" [note:𐎋𐎐𐎊𐎚 • (knyt) “delicate, tender”]. Notes: while it is suggested that the name roughly translates to “mountain of covering” the double kn is not explained as opposed to the knn root. This spelling may be a way to differentiate Canaan (kn’n) from the mountain or may indicate a twin peaked area similar to Mt. Mashu(the twin(ned) Mountains). [note: upon further consideration; Ba’al was found in Dabr-land (modern day Daburiyya) on the instructions of Kothar, therefore I believe the mountain range in question is Mount Tabor, another twin peaked region that would lead to the underworld. Knkn may be etymologically related to Kafr Kanna from Proto-Semitic *kawan, Akkadian: 𒄀 (kânum) “kânum/qanû. (G, a-u, durative ikân, perfect iktūn, preterite ikūn, imperative kūn) (from Old Akkadian on) to stay in place, remain stationary, to remain steadfast and secure, to endure, to be true, to be loyal/ with Tabor possibly being link to the akk. tappûm/tappû for companion/ mesopatmain taba 𒋰𒁀]
